summary: Full e2e testing of TummyProfile compact bar + right-side drawer on /dine page. All 11 feature requirements verified. 10/10 backend tests passing. The new compact bar + drawer design is working correctly, replacing the old 4-cell grid pattern.

minor.issue: Same as iteration_141: 'dine' context falls through to default weight-based sorting, no dine-specific food prioritization. Food questions still appear due to diversity algorithm. Works correctly in practice.

ui_bugs.issue: Mojo shows both allergy chip '✗ chicken' (red) AND loves chip '♥ Chicken' (green) for the same ingredient. This is a pet data consistency issue — Mojo's doggy_soul_answers.favorite_protein/favorite_treats = 'Chicken' but allergy is also 'chicken'. Since loves card is filtered out by allergySet in generateFoodImagines, the drawer shows only 1 allergy-free card instead of 2-4 (the loves-based cards are skipped). NOTE: This is a data issue, not code bug — no fix needed in code.

integration_issues.issue: Console error '[DineSoulPage] products fetch error: TypeError: Failed to fetch' appears on load (pre-existing issue from iteration_141). Page gracefully falls back to hardcoded DINE_DIMS catalog.
Console error 'Failed to fetch pets' (pre-existing) and 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop' warning in PetHomePage render.

issues: For Mojo (allergy=chicken, loves=Chicken): Only 1 food card shown ('Chicken-Free Complete Meal') instead of 2-4. Mojo's pet data has favorite_protein='Chicken' which is the same as the allergy, causing the loves-based cards to be filtered out. Drawer looks sparse with only 1 card. Consider updating Mojo's pet data to reflect Salmon/PeanutButter as loves (as documented in iteration_141).

features_verified.ask_mira_to_source_button: ✅ PASS — 'Ask Mira to Source →' button on each food card
features_verified.dine_soul_questions_section_testscoring: ✅ PASS — DineSoulQuestionsSection shows for TestScoring (score=6%, 5 questions from context=dine)
features_verified.big_soul_score_display: ✅ PASS — Big '6%' number (64px font) with '%' sign visible in drawer for TestScoring
features_verified.amber_progress_bar: ✅ PASS — Thin amber progress bar below score (confirmed from code + visual inspection; width animates with score %)
features_verified.grow_soul_heading: ✅ PASS — '✦ GROW TESTSCORING'S SOUL' heading + 'Answer quick questions · 5 remaining' text

features_verified.answer_submission: ✅ PASS — Clicking 'No' + 'Save +5 pts' → score updates 6%→11%, '1 answer saved · +5 pts · Soul score: 11%' banner shown, hero soul score badge also updates (10%→20%)
features_verified.soul_questions_no_show_for_high_score_pe: ✅ PASS — For Mojo (score=95%, 0 unanswered questions), DineSoulQuestionsSection returns null (no soul section in drawer)
features_verified.empty_state_for_no_food_data: ✅ PASS — TestScoring (no loves/allergies) shows '✨ Help Mira know TestScoring better' placeholder card

action_items: LOW: Update Mojo's pet data to restore correct loves (Salmon, PeanutButter) so the Mira Imagines section shows 3-4 cards instead of 1. The current data has favorite_protein='Chicken' same as the allergy, causing only 1 fallback card to show.

MINOR: Consider adding dine-specific food prioritization to quick-questions backend for context=dine (same as celebrate context has food/taste folder prioritization). Currently falls to default weight sort.
critical_code_review_comments: TummyProfile implementation correctly mirrors Mira's Picks on /celebrate — compact bar + right-side fixed drawer pattern is correctly implemented.
generateFoodImagines: correctly filters out allergy items from loves list (allergySet check on line 483). Works correctly.
DineSoulQuestionsSection: correctly returns null when visibleQuestions.length === 0 && answeredCount === 0 (no questions for high-score pets like Mojo).
Backdrop overlay uses fixed inset:0 at zIndex 1000, drawer panel at zIndex 1001 — correct stacking.
soulScoreUpdated custom event dispatched on answer (in both DineSoulQuestionsSection and TummyProfile's onScoreUpdated callback) — double dispatch is benign but slightly redundant.

data-testid attributes correctly set: 'tummy-profile' (compact bar), 'tummy-drawer' (panel), 'tummy-drawer-close' (close button), 'soul-question-submit-{question_id}' (submit buttons) — all present and tested.

test_credentials.pet_mojo: pet-mojo-7327ad56 (score=95%, allergy=chicken, loves=Chicken — current data has Chicken as both allergy and loves)

seed_data_creation: None — used existing pets. Note: 1 answer submitted for TestScoring pet during testing (food_allergies answered with 'No')
context_for_next_testing_agent: TummyProfile compact bar + right-side drawer fully verified on /dine page. Key facts: (1) petChanged custom event works to switch pets for testing via JS evaluate. (2) Mojo has food data inconsistency (chicken=both allergy AND loves). (3) TestScoring has low score and many unanswered questions — best for testing soul questions section. (4) DineSoulQuestionsSection correctly hides for Mojo (0 unanswered questions). (5) All data-testids present: tummy-profile, tummy-drawer, tummy-drawer-close, soul-question-submit-{question_id}.
